title: Pure Batch Cron (No Endpoints)
description: ARCHETYPE: batch_job. Shape: backend-only, the ENTIRE app is one scheduled cron block — no HTTP endpoints, no pages, no auth. This is the purest form of batch_job. Schedule: every day at '03:00 AM': find and delete Sessions older than 7 days. ONE auxiliary endpoint GET /api/session-count so tests can verify rows exist (required for test runner) — but the CORE of this task is the cron block. DO NOT add endpoints for creating sessions. DO NOT add dashboards. DO NOT add auth. The classifier should see: cron + delete + minimal reads = batch_job.
skeleton:
build for javascript backend

create a Sessions table:
  user_id, required
  token, required
  created_at_date, auto

# Your nightly prune job + GET /api/session-count endpoint here.
# Use: every day at '03:00 AM': then delete old Sessions.
# Then ONE endpoint: GET /api/session-count that returns a count.
